response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack and distributed systems engineering skills, with exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and event-driven architectures. However, the resume lacks explicit evidence of executive-level leadership, multi-agent AI production experience in fintech, or scaling engineering teams to 30+ people—key requirements for this CTO role.
strengths: Diverse technical background with experience in distributed systems, microservices, and event-driven architectures (e.g., GISCollective, Dunnhumby).
Hands-on contributions to AI-agent tooling (e.g., MCP code generators at re:cinq) and open-source projects.
Strong problem-solving and craftsmanship mindset, with a focus on testability, observability, and user experience.
concerns: No explicit executive leadership experience or track record scaling engineering teams to the required size (10→30+).
Agentic AI experience appears limited to tooling/frameworks rather than production multi-agent systems in regulated environments.
Fintech experience is tangential (e.g., pricing optimization at Optilyz) and lacks depth in credit systems or real-time risk modeling.
